they correctly identify the code as auto-generated and benign.\n- No logical flaws or reasoning errors are evident.\n- The reports appropriately note the absence of suspicious activity.\n\n**Scores assigned:**\n- Malware: consistently 0 across all reports, justified by the lack of malicious behavior.\n- Obfuscated: 0, correctly indicating no obfuscation.\n- Risk: very low (~0.1–0.2), aligned with the benign nature of the code.\n\n**Justification for high risk or malware scores:**\n- Since the code is purely an API client with no malicious activity, scores of 0 are appropriate.\n- No claims or evidence suggest any higher risk.\n\n---\n\n### Critical Evaluation:\n\n- **Are the request parameters or response handling suspicious?** No. They are straightforward, validated, and typical for API clients.\n- **Are there any hidden backdoors or malicious code?** No.\n- **Is there any obfuscation or dynamic code execution?** No.\n- **Are sensitive data or secrets hardcoded?** No, only standard request parameters and enums.\n- **Is there any sign of supply chain compromise?** No, the code is auto-generated and appears standard.\n\n### Final assessment:\n\n**Malware score:** 0 — no evidence of malicious code or behavior.  \n**Obfuscated score:** 0 — code is clear and straightforward.  \n**Risk score:** 0.1–0.2 — very low, consistent with benign API client code.\n\n---\n\n### **Summary and recommendations:**\n\n- The code is a typical, auto-generated API client with no malicious intent.\n- The high confidence scores and low malware/obfuscation/risk scores are justified.\n- No changes needed;
